summary: DHH describes a warranty dispute with Officeline, an official Apple reseller in Denmark, over a faulty DVD drive in his 17-inch iMac. The machine is 19 months old, outside Apple's 1-year warranty but within the 2-year complaint period mandated by Danish consumer protection law. He contrasts his positive experience with Apple's direct warranty service against the poor treatment from their authorized reseller. The post is a consumer warning advising readers to avoid purchasing Apple equipment from Officeline.
key_points: Official Apple resellers may not honor consumer rights as well as Apple directly
Danish law provides a 2-year warranty period that exceeds Apple's standard 1-year coverage
Consumer protection laws can be a valuable tool when manufacturers' warranties expire
Brand reputation can be undermined by poor authorized reseller experiences
money_quotes: While I've been extremely pleased with the warranty from Apple, their official resellers are quite another story.
key_insight: A brand's reputation is only as strong as its weakest authorized reseller, and consumers should know their legal rights extend beyond manufacturer warranties.
